返回日报
Hacker News #4

谁在害怕中国模型？

Who's afraid of Chinese models?

AI 作者 mfiguiere 热度 970 回复 874 2026/07/20 19:05
打开原帖 打开外部链接 JSON

摘要

  • Listen to this post:
  • There’s a story I tell about my first day in STRT-431 at Kellogg School of Management, the introductory class that every first-year MBA was required to take; I leafed through the readings and case studies and was dismayed that there weren’t any tech companies on the docket. Me being me, I spoke to the professor after class wondering why, and was told that the goal of the course was not to necessarily learn about specific industries, but rather to uncover broadly applicable universal principles that could be applied to any company in any industry.

命中 2 个 AI/科技关键词。

正文翻译

暂无正文

暂无原文

回复翻译 20

astrobiased 2026/07/21 18:40
这篇文章切中要害，在安全性方面对模型的访问不平等。我正在开发 OSS，其中安全性对用户来说很重要......但是像 GPT 5.6 和 Fable 这样的前沿模型会崩溃并声明我无法获取信息/访问权限。

这是极其不平衡的，我必须诉诸 GLM 5.2/K3 来确保这些安全问题（希望）得到正确解决。

对于 OSS，这是我经历过的最违反直觉的体验之一。我比以往任何时候都更加相信开放权重和开放管道模型对于人工智能和社会前沿的进步 100% 至关重要。

The post hits it spot on with unequal access to the models in terms of security. I'm developing OSS where security is important for the user ... but the frontier models like GPT 5.6 and Fable flake out and state that I cannot get the info/access.

This is extremely lopsided I'll have to resort to GLM 5.2/K3 to ensure that those security issues (hopefully) are resolved properly.

For OSS, this is one of the most counterintuitive experiences I have ever had. More than ever I'm convinced that open weight and open pipelines models are 100% critical for progress on the AI and societal fronts.

楼层
lennart-rth 2026/07/22 01:57
是的，限制美国模型的好处绝对超过了这些模型可能对 OSS 社区产生的积极影响！
Yeah, the benefit of restricting us models is definitely outweighed by the positive effect these models could have for the OSS community!
楼层
bigyabai 2026/07/22 01:28
我部分在想，美国政府是不是在封杀Anthropic和OpenAI的嘴，以便他们囤积NOBUS漏洞利用： https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NOBUS

如果这些模型能用来修补（或发现）危险的有效载荷，那么限制这些模型就会有相当大的动机。在像Windows或Chrome这样的大型项目中，可能仍然有数十个未修补的漏洞，这些漏洞太微妙，较小的模型无法捕捉到。

Part of me wonders if the US Government is muzzling Anthropic and OpenAI so they can stockpile NOBUS exploits: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NOBUS

There would be a decently large incentive to restrict these models if they could be used to patch (or discover) dangerous payloads. In larger projects like Windows or Chrome, there might still be dozens of unpatched exploits that are too subtle to catch with smaller models.

楼层
wanick 2026/07/22 03:05
汉隆剃须刀。并不是美国国家安全局压制了任何人，而是律师们害怕糟糕的标题。同样的结果，愚蠢得多的理由。
Hanlon's razor. It's not the NSA muzzling anyone, it's lawyers terrified of a bad headline. Same outcome, much dumber reason.
楼层
HeatrayEnjoyer 2026/07/22 03:38
这可能有多种原因。不要忘记，恶意行为者喜欢躲在 Hanlon 的后面。
It can be multiple reasons. And don't forget that malicious actors love hiding behind Hanlon's.
楼层
rnd0 2026/07/22 02:23
当然是 - 这是不言而喻的。
Of course they are -it goes without saying.
楼层
linkregister 2026/07/22 01:52
NOBUS 漏洞很少引起民选官员的兴趣。贸易限制和互惠更加明显和清晰。大多数民选官员几乎不知道 NOBUS 漏洞的含义。

即使在斯诺登之前的美国网络霸权全盛时期，这些功能也几乎不是白宫官员思考过程的一部分。

NOBUS exploits have rarely been a driving interest for elected officials. Trade restrictions and reciprocity are far more salient and legible. Most elected officials are only barely aware of what NOBUS exploits even mean.

Even during the pre-Snowden heyday of US cyber supremacy, these capabilities were barely part of the thought process of White House officials.

楼层
bigyabai 2026/07/22 01:57
相反，美国现在比以往任何时候都更深入地卷入不对称战争。美国的系统正在被中国的“盐台风”等攻击所利用，并引发了相互攻击的问题。美国软实力的其他目标，例如伊朗（Stuxnet 受害者）正在升级其黑客攻击，并利用中国技术来扼杀美国的指挥和控制。

我可以相信 NOBUS 和其他后门被忽视很长一段时间，但我很难相信它被现任政府忽视。

Conversely, the United States is now embroiled deeper in asymmetric warfare than ever before. US-based systems are being exploited by Chinese efforts like Salt Typhoon and raising questions about reciprocal attacks. Other targets of US soft-power like Iran (Stuxnet victim) are escalating their hacking efforts and using Chinese technology to stifle American command and control.

I can believe that NOBUS and other backdoors were ignored for a long time, but I have a hard time believing that it's being ignored by the current administration.

楼层

楼层
ballon_monkey 2026/07/21 11:18
人们需要记住两件事：

1) 中国可以（并且确实）使用这些模型来影响西方。他们接受有关台湾和香港的虚假信息的训练。或者假装历史对中国有利。

2）忽略包含虚假信息的模型，它们是令人难以置信的。但您应该害怕直接通过模型创建者运行推理。如果您认为您的数据与通过美国的模型提供商（无论是前沿还是像 fireworks.ai 这样的模型主机）运行相比是安全的，那么请让我知道您的银行详细信息，以便我可以四处查看。

The 2 things people need to remember:

1) China can (and does) use the models to influence the west. They train in false information about Taiwan and Hong Kong. Or pretend like history is in favor of China.

2) Ignoring the models containing false information, they are incredible. But you should be scared of running inference via the model creators directly. If you think your data is safe compared to running it via model providers in the US ( either frontier or model hosts like fireworks.ai ) then please let me know your bank details so I can poke around.

楼层
w4lker 2026/07/21 17:32
https://thereallo.dev/blog/claude-code-prompt-steganography

我为什么要更信任一家美国公司而不是中国公司呢？

https://thereallo.dev/blog/claude-code-prompt-steganography

Why should I trust a US company more than a Chinese one?

楼层
theptip 2026/07/21 22:57
您的展品明显是一种反蒸馏技术。在监管较松的公司那里，情况可能会糟糕得多。
Your exhibit is an obvious anti-distillation technique. You can expect far worse from less-regulated companies.
楼层
throw1234567891 2026/07/22 20:13
Anthropic 和 OpenAI 均不受“受监管业务”含义的监管。
Neither Anthropic nor OpenAI are regulated in the "regulated business" meaning.
楼层
butlike 2026/07/21 23:03
正是我的问题
Exactly my question
楼层
jayd16 2026/07/21 23:15
你也不应该相信这两者，但这就是Whataboutism。
You shouldn't trust either but also this is whataboutism.
楼层
WithinReason 2026/07/21 23:36
不，不是。比较主义和Whataboutism是有区别的
No it's not. There is a difference between a comparison and whataboutism
楼层
pessimizer 2026/07/22 01:26
不，不是。 Whataboutism 是（由冷战时期的宣传者）创建的一个特定术语，用于指代美国批评苏联有关“X”的问题，而苏联则通过询问美国的“Y”来答复。

这个术语不是用于指代某些美国人批评美国任何官方敌人的“X”，而其他人则提醒您美国也完全做“X”的情况。

No, it is not. Whataboutism is a specific term created (by Cold War propagandists) to refer to when the US criticizes the USSR about "X," and the Soviet Union replies by asking about "Y" in the US.

It is not a term created to refer to when some American criticizes any of America's official enemies for "X," and someone else reminds you that America also does exactly "X."

楼层
jayd16 2026/07/22 01:35
> “Whataboutism”或“whataboutery”（如“但是X呢？”）是指用反指控来回应指控的宣传策略，而不是对原始指控进行解释或辩护。

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Whataboutism

这是反指控，而不是辩护，因此，什么主义。

> "Whataboutism" or "whataboutery" (as in, "but what about X?") refers to the propaganda strategy of responding to an accusation with a counter-accusation instead of offering an explanation or defense against the original accusation.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Whataboutism

This is a counter accusation and not a defense, ergo, whataboutism.

楼层

楼层
rq1 2026/07/22 03:04
我从来不明白人们如何提及或理解“whataboutsim”。

我个人认为这不是一种转移注意力，而是对第一个指控所暗示的道德更高点的批评。换句话说：保持你自己的房子井然有序。

I never understood how people referred to or understood “whataboutsim”.

I personally understand it not as a diversion but as a critique of the moral higher ground implied by the first accusation. Said differently: keep your own house in order.

楼层
